items.title: Canon + ADR convergence for Postgres/Supabase target
items.next_action: Converge canonical docs to ADR-0001 and enforce engine-switch governance gates.
items.acceptance_criteria: ADR exists as DECISION_CONFIRMED, canonical docs reference it, and engine-switch without ADR is blocked by gate.

items.title: Tooling parity foundation (Supabase Local + CI Postgres scaffold)
items.next_action: Add deterministic local bootstrap and CI Postgres parity smoke without runtime cutover.
items.acceptance_criteria: Supabase local bootstrap script exists, CI Postgres parity smoke exists, and DB-only scope guard passes.

items.title: Gate portability for mixed dialect table declarations
items.next_action: Ensure ownership drift and inventory gates handle mysqlTable and pgTable during mixed-dialect transition.
items.acceptance_criteria: Manifest ownership drift gate parses mysqlTable and pgTable declarations and stays deterministic.

items.title: Ask ISA Postgres schema subset and migration path
items.next_action: Implement Postgres subset for Ask ISA substrate tables with isolated migration history.
items.acceptance_criteria: Postgres migrations for Ask ISA subset apply on empty DB and smoke data path is queryable.

items.title: Ask ISA parity gates on Postgres
items.next_action: Enforce citation presence/validity and groundedness thresholds in Postgres parity runs.
items.acceptance_criteria: Ask ISA parity gate shows citation presence=1 and citation validity=1 with non-regressive abstention correctness.

items.title: ESRS Mapping Postgres schema subset and migration path
items.next_action: Implement Postgres subset for mapping and feedback tables while preserving current contracts.
items.acceptance_criteria: Subset migrations apply and baseline smoke checks for mapping tables pass.

items.title: ESRS Mapping parity gates on Postgres
items.next_action: Run negative fixture and authority-correctness checks against Postgres parity path.
items.acceptance_criteria: Negative fixtures remain negative and authority correctness is non-regressive against baseline.

items.title: Advisory parity + normalized filter model
items.next_action: Migrate advisory substrate and replace JSON hot-path filtering with normalized join-table access.
items.acceptance_criteria: Advisory version/retrieval flows stay reproducible and filter queries use join-based indexed paths.

items.title: Rehydration and deterministic rebuild harness
items.next_action: Implement source-manifest driven rebuild scripts for deterministic empty-DB bootstrap.
items.acceptance_criteria: Two consecutive rebuild runs on empty Postgres are idempotent (no semantic drift).

items.title: CI parity + limited runtime seam
items.next_action: Keep mysql runtime default while Postgres parity jobs and seam controls prove non-regressive behavior.
items.acceptance_criteria: CI parity jobs are green and parity drift reports are explicit/reproducible.

items.title: Cutover readiness review
items.next_action: Run objective go/no-go review based on journey parity, rebuild reliability, and gate portability.
items.acceptance_criteria: Readiness checklist is complete and signed with no unresolved hard-fail parity regressions.

items.title: Runtime cutover to Postgres default
items.next_action: Flip runtime default to Postgres after readiness sign-off; retain explicit rollback path.
items.acceptance_criteria: Postgres is default runtime, mysql path is legacy fallback, rollback path is documented and tested.

items.title: Promote ESRS_MAPPING to the canonical decision core with confidence-calibrated gap analysis and attribute recommendation outputs
items.next_action: Formalize the decision-core contract in code and docs so gap analysis and GS1 attribute recommendation become the primary value engine above CATALOG and KNOWLEDGE_BASE.
items.acceptance_criteria: ESRS_MAPPING outputs expose stable, evidence-backed decision artefacts for gap analysis and attribute recommendation; architecture, runtime contract, and validation references stay aligned.
items.source_plan: ISA technical documentation canon and synthesized target architecture (2026-03-04)

items.title: Make ADVISORY the canonical stakeholder-deliverable layer with structured outputs, diffing, and PDF-grade export
items.next_action: Build durable advisory artefacts that package mapping, gap, and change-intelligence outputs into governed stakeholder deliverables.
items.acceptance_criteria: ADVISORY produces versioned structured outputs and shareable report artefacts that trace back to upstream capabilities.
items.source_plan: ISA technical documentation canon and synthesized target architecture (2026-03-04)

items.title: Wire NEWS_HUB into the compliance cockpit as change intelligence instead of a standalone news surface
items.next_action: Prioritize flows that turn news and regulatory change into updated compliance priorities, deadlines, and downstream advisory triggers.
items.acceptance_criteria: NEWS_HUB surfaces expose change-intelligence artefacts that can be consumed by dashboard, decision-core, and advisory layers.
items.source_plan: ISA technical documentation canon and synthesized target architecture (2026-03-04)

items.title: Stand up the decision-quality gold set and benchmark harness for ESRS_MAPPING, ASK_ISA, and ADVISORY
items.next_action: Convert the research synthesis into an initial gold set, rubric set, and schema-validated evaluation harness so ISA can measure mapping correctness, advisory quality, and evidence-bound answer quality before further feature expansion.
items.acceptance_criteria: A first decision-grade benchmark suite exists with gold-set categories, executable fixtures, and schema-validated result artifacts for ESRS mapping, advisory outputs, and grounded Q&A.
items.source_plan: External research synthesis (2026-03-04) translated into the canonical execution queue

items.title: Formalize the confidence and abstention contract for ESRS_MAPPING outputs and downstream ADVISORY packaging
items.next_action: Define how confidence score, confidence basis, uncertainty class, abstention, and review thresholds are represented so decision artifacts become decision-grade rather than merely plausible.
items.acceptance_criteria: ESRS_MAPPING and ADVISORY expose a stable confidence contract with explicit evidence basis, uncertainty handling, and review/escalation semantics backed by code and tests.
items.source_plan: External research synthesis (2026-03-04) translated into the canonical execution queue

items.title: Automate provenance verification and citation integrity gates across CATALOG and KNOWLEDGE_BASE
items.next_action: Move from provenance metadata being merely present to provenance verification being enforced through content-hash, verification timestamp, source authenticity, and citation-integrity checks.
items.acceptance_criteria: Ingest and retrieval paths enforce strong provenance fields and citation-integrity checks, with deterministic verification artifacts that downstream capabilities can trust.
items.source_plan: External research synthesis (2026-03-04) translated into the canonical execution queue

items.title: Model regulatory events, deadline extraction, and downstream advisory triggers in NEWS_HUB
items.next_action: Define and implement a regulatory event primitive that distinguishes authoritative change, guidance, consultation, enforcement, and supporting news so NEWS_HUB can trigger downstream roadmap and advisory updates.
items.acceptance_criteria: NEWS_HUB exposes a typed event model with deadline and significance fields that can drive change-intelligence surfaces and downstream advisory triggers.
items.source_plan: External research synthesis (2026-03-04) translated into the canonical execution queue

items.title: Operationalize OpenTelemetry spans, SLO artifacts, and regression alerts for evidence-backed ISA flows
items.next_action: Turn the existing SLO and telemetry docs into real runtime instrumentation and regression signaling for ASK_ISA, NEWS_HUB, ESRS_MAPPING, and ADVISORY-critical paths.
items.acceptance_criteria: Decision, retrieval, and change-intelligence paths emit actionable telemetry; SLO artifacts and regression alerts are backed by real runtime signals rather than draft-only docs.
items.source_plan: External research synthesis (2026-03-04) translated into the canonical execution queue

items.title: Prioritize authoritative source expansion for EFRAG taxonomy, EU regulatory feeds, and GS1 Digital Link where ingestion leverage is highest
items.next_action: Use authority, machine-readability, and maintenance burden to select the next highest-leverage ingest targets instead of expanding data sources opportunistically.
items.acceptance_criteria: A small, reviewable source-expansion slice is chosen and justified against authority, ingestibility, maintenance burden, and expected impact on mapping or change-intelligence quality.
items.source_plan: External research synthesis (2026-03-04) translated into the canonical execution queue

items.title: Security waiver burn-down: remove temporary advisory waivers from high-severity gate policy (SEC-002)
items.next_action: PARKED during ISA implementation-only mode. Resume security waiver burn-down after ISA v2 (Phase A-D) maturity declaration and controlled unpause.
items.acceptance_criteria: security-gate passes with SECURITY_AUDIT_BLOCKING_LEVELS=high,critical and empty waiver IDs; architecture/backlog updated.

items.title: Security ratchet follow-up: evaluate high-severity advisory blocking mode (SEC-001)
items.next_action: Completed in SEC-001 high-severity ratchet patch group.
items.acceptance_criteria: Security policy/risk posture is updated with explicit high-severity handling decision and backlog tasks; deterministic security gate behavior preserved.

items.title: Production readiness convergence PG-02: harden six runtime contracts and architecture deltas
items.next_action: Completed in PG-02 runtime contract hardening.
items.acceptance_criteria: All six runtime contracts are non-DRAFT, contain only evidence-backed claims or explicit UNKNOWN; canonical doc-code validation passes.

items.title: Lock canonical plan-as-code entrypoints
items.next_action: Ensure NEXT_ACTIONS.json and BACKLOG.csv are referenced by AGENT_START_HERE and AGENT_MAP
items.acceptance_criteria: AGENT_START_HERE and AGENT_MAP link to docs/planning/NEXT_ACTIONS.json and docs/planning/BACKLOG.csv

items.title: Define manual preflight checklist (NO_GATES window)
items.next_action: Add a minimal manual preflight checklist in docs/planning/INDEX.md and ensure AGENT_MAP points to it (no CI gates during window)
items.acceptance_criteria: docs/planning/INDEX.md contains a manual preflight checklist; docs/agent/AGENT_MAP.md points to it; NEXT_ACTIONS contains no gate-command references (NO_GATES window)

items.title: Finalize ISA Core Contract (6 capabilities + non-scope)
items.next_action: Edit docs/core/ISA_CORE_CONTRACT.md to lock the 6 core capabilities, explicit non-scope, and expected entrypoints placeholders; ensure AGENT_MAP references it
items.acceptance_criteria: ISA_CORE_CONTRACT lists 6 capabilities and explicit non-scope; referenced from docs/agent/AGENT_MAP.md; gates pass

items.title: Create minimal specs for all 6 core capabilities
items.next_action: Create docs/spec/INDEX.md plus 6 minimal specs (ASK_ISA, NEWS_HUB, KNOWLEDGE_BASE, CATALOG, ESRS_MAPPING, ADVISORY) and link them from INDEX and AGENT_MAP
items.acceptance_criteria: Each spec exists, includes inputs/outputs and acceptance criteria; all are linked from docs/spec/INDEX.md and docs/agent/AGENT_MAP.md; gates pass

items.title: Ask ISA: define end-to-end runtime contract and entrypoints (server+client)
acceptance_criteria: docs/spec/ASK_ISA.md links to proven code entrypoints (server/prompts/ask_isa/*, client UI refs)
A single documented request/response contract exists (paths + minimal examples)
A minimal server route or callable function is identified and referenced from docs

items.title: Ask ISA: minimal smoke harness (no gates yet) and manual runbook
acceptance_criteria: A single command is documented to run an Ask ISA smoke query locally
docs/governance/MANUAL_PREFLIGHT.md references the smoke step as optional validation

items.title: News Hub: baseline architecture map and proven code/data entrypoints
acceptance_criteria: docs/spec/NEWS_HUB.md links to proven code/data entrypoints
A minimal end-to-end baseline is defined (ingest -> store -> expose -> UI)
No duplicate planning sources created; all tracked in NEXT_ACTIONS.json
